Assembly: The assembly cmRicSoro2.1 is based on 51x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Order and Orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome 1 between ~9.69-9.94Mb, Chromosome 2 between ~45.63-46.24Mb, Chromosome 3 between ~12.17-12.65Mb and 39.46-39.86Mb, Chromosome 4 between ~17.22-18.20Mb, Chromosome 5 between ~17.96-18.24Mb and Chromosome 8 between ~5.17-5.58Mb is unsure.

Assembly: The assembly icMicPygm2.1 is based on 274x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi and OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (13,712 - 15,573 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 2 (13,291 - 14,760 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 3 (15,994 - 19,128 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 4 (12,850- 13,937 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 5 (13,575 - 15,445 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 6 (11,312 - 15,191 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 7 (12,027 - 13,300 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 8 (14,397 - 15,671 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 9 (10,470 - 12,272 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome X (13,700 - 15,044 Kbp) are unknown. The X and Y chromosomes were identified by read coverage.The Hi-C signal suggests a heterozygous reciprocal translocation between Chromosome 4 (28,521?30,059 Kbp) and Chromosome 9 (0?5,386 Kbp).A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences.

Assembly: The assembly ilClySyri1.hap2.1 is based on 61x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Assembly: The assembly of Rochia nilotica (xgRocNilo1) is based on 41X PacBio data and 201X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr). The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. No contigs were found to be contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 179 regions totaling 26.247 Mb (with the largest being 2.144 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. Mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. During manual curation, 6 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 3,384,646 pb (with the largest being 874,703 pb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly iiTriMino1.hap1.1 is based on 25x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The Z chromosome was identified based on copy number in the diploid assembly and HiC signal. The order and orientation of contigs along chromosome 9 is uncertain from the start to 0.87 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly wpEulXant1.hap1.1 is based on 50x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Order and orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome 2 from ~46.78-62.06Mb is unsure. There are haplotypic inversions on Chromosome 1 from ~75.35-81.85Mb, Chromosome 6 from ~26.35-48.63Mb, Chromosome 11 from ~96.13-107.70Mb, Chromosome 13 from ~81.99-87.56Mb and Chromosome 16 from ~0.71-6.07Mb.
